faq.q: What PWM frequency should I use for servos?
faq.a: Standard servos use 50 Hz. Modern digital servos respond better at 200–300 Hz for improved response time.
Historical convention from the first servos. Modern digital servos perform better at higher frequencies like 200–300 Hz.

faq.q: Can I use extended pulse widths to get more than 180°?
faq.a: Some servos accept 0.5–2.5 ms pulses to cover 270° or more. Always check your servo's datasheet for supported range.

faq.q: How do I control servos with Arduino?
faq.a: Use the Servo.h library. The servo.write(degrees) command handles PWM automatically.

faq.q: What's the difference between torque and speed?
faq.a: Datasheets list torque in kg·cm and speed in sec/60°. A 20 kg·cm servo is industrial-grade; 1–3 kg·cm is hobby-grade.
